reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures IMO SL 2011 C6. (1) "Positive integer n" → ℕ+. (2) Periodic word over {a,b} → W : ℤ → Fin 2. (3) "Minimal period N > 2^n" → hN : 2^n.1 < N combined with hW : Function.Periodic W N and hW' : no smaller positive integer is a period — correctly captures minimality. (4) "appears": the def ∃ k, ∀ i : Fin U.1, U.2 i = W (k+i) encodes U = x_k...x_{k+|U|−1}. (5) "ubiquitous": Fin.snoc with 0/1 produces Ua, Ub; Fin.cons with 0/1 produces aU, bU; all required to appear. (6) "At least n ubiquitous finite nonempty words": encoded as an embedding Fin n ↪ (Σ k, Fin k → Fin 2) (giving n distinct elements) with constraints (x i).1 ≠ 0 (nonempty) and ubiquitous W (x i). The embedding form is logically equivalent to "there exist n distinct...", so no drift in strength.
